introSection: The National Amusement Park is an amusement park in Sükhbaatar District, Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The park was opened in 1969. On 5 July 2005, the governor and mayor of Ulaanbaatar City Government issued a decree on granting a 15-year lease of land to the amusement park.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.content: The park includes a castle, roller coaster, games, paddle boats and haunted house. Currently, it has 24 rides in total. It also features an ice skating rink during winter.

sections.title: Rides and attractions
sections.content: The park has 1 currently operating roller coaster, and 1 defunct coaster.

fullText:
The National Amusement Park is an amusement park in Sükhbaatar District, Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia.

The park was opened in 1969. On 5 July 2005, the governor and mayor of Ulaanbaatar City Government issued a decree on granting a 15-year lease of land to the amusement park.

The park includes a castle, roller coaster, games, paddle boats and haunted house. Currently, it has 24 rides in total. It also features an ice skating rink during winter.

The park has 1 currently operating roller coaster, and 1 defunct coaster.


 * Tourism in Mongolia
 * Red Rock Castle
